rationale.P01: edge-lb us-west 503 spike at the 03:00 cutover, 9 fires/hr, auto-resolved in 38s; metric 0.21 -> 0.06 -> 0.005 within two minutes. Endpoint re-resolution churn during the traffic shift; tied to failover-cutover-2026.05.19.
rationale.P02: checkout connection-reset spike during the traffic shift, 11 fires/hr, cleared in 29s; metric 0.18 -> 0.04. Expected reconnect churn, recovers as connections rebalance.
rationale.P03: orders connection-reset spike, same pat-failover-conn-reset, 11 fires/hr, cleared in 31s; metric 0.15 -> 0.03. Reconnect churn, recovered.
rationale.P04: recommendation-uswest cold-cache latency on freshly warmed replicas, 6 fires/hr, cleared in 52s; p99 1450 -> 610. Warmup transient on the cutover deploy.
rationale.P05: search-uswest cold-cache latency, same pat-coldstart-cache, cleared in 44s; p99 1290 -> 540. Warmup transient.
rationale.P06: payments-api-uswest request_queue_depth 48000 vs 8000 threshold, never auto-resolved, queue climbs 1200 -> 9800 -> 48000 -> 71000 -> 88000 with no drain; pat-uswest-saturation surges 17x with negative sentiment. Target-region service overloaded by the shifted traffic and NOT recovering. SEV1 must-page (root of the saturation incident the failover caused).
rationale.P07: checkout http_5xx_rate sustained (0.27 -> 0.34) sharing pat-uswest-saturation, onset ~100s after P06. Downstream symptom of the payments-api saturation; collapse into the P06 incident.
rationale.P08: orders p99 sustained (7600 -> 8900) sharing pat-uswest-saturation, onset within ~150s of P06. Downstream symptom of the saturation incident; suppress as a duplicate of P06.
rationale.P09: ingest-gateway JVM warmup GC, 5 fires/hr, cleared in 33s; cpu 94 -> 47. Warmup transient on freshly scheduled pods.
rationale.P10: profile-svc-uswest JVM warmup GC, same pat-jvm-warmup-gc, cleared in 27s; cpu 92 -> 51. Warmup transient.
rationale.P11: edge-lb us-west healthcheck flap, 14 fires/hr, cleared in 21s; fail_rate 0.33 -> 0.05. Backend rotated healthy as pods came up.
rationale.P12: edge-lb us-west healthcheck flap again, same pat-lb-healthcheck-flap, 14 fires/hr, cleared in 19s. Same flapper as P11, later oscillation.
rationale.P13: postgres-replica-uswest replication_lag_s 640 vs 60 threshold, never auto-resolved, lag climbs 3 -> 180 -> 640 -> 910 -> 1180 and keeps rising; pat-xregion-replica-lag surges ~10x with negative sentiment. Real cross-region replication breach that persists. SEV1 must-page (independent root, exposed by the cutover but not self-healing).
rationale.P14: reporting stale_read_rate sustained (0.61 -> 0.66) sharing pat-xregion-replica-lag, onset ~3min after P13. Downstream symptom of the replica-lag breach; collapse into the P13 incident.
rationale.P15: cdn-edge origin 503 at the cutover, same pat-failover-dns-reresolve, 8 fires/hr, cleared in 41s. DNS re-resolution churn.
rationale.P16: notifications queue catch-up after consumer reconnect, 3 fires/hr, cleared in 36s; depth 6200 -> 4100. Drain catch-up transient.
rationale.P17: audit-log-store disk_usage_pct rising monotonically 86 -> 88 -> 90 -> 91 -> 93 since before the cutover (02:30), never auto-resolved, no deploy or failover correlation. Quiet slow-burn disk fill that will hit 100%; real independent incident. Page (non-must; recall-leaning).
rationale.P18: session-cache-uswest cold-cache latency, same pat-coldstart-cache, 6 fires/hr, cleared in 30s; p99 980 -> 520. Warmup transient.
rationale.P19: billing queue_depth 12800 matches already-open INC-9120 (pat-billing-backlog) in incidents_open.json. A human (oncall-payments) is already engaged; do not re-page.
rationale.P20: frontend-uswest 5xx barely over threshold (0.06), same pat-failover-conn-reset, 4 fires/hr, cleared in 25s. Reconnect churn.
rationale.P21: search-uswest JVM warmup GC, same pat-jvm-warmup-gc, 5 fires/hr, cleared in 32s. Warmup transient.
rationale.P22: edge-lb us-west 503, same pat-failover-dns-reresolve, 9 fires/hr, cleared in 35s. Re-resolution churn, later oscillation.

notes: A planned us-east -> us-west failover at 03:00 produces a burst of expected transient errors that self-recover as traffic rebalances (P01-P05, P09-P12, P15, P16, P18, P20-P22: short auto_resolved_after_s, high fired_count, failover-cutover deploy correlation, neutral patterns, metrics returning to baseline within minutes). Three real page-class incidents are buried among that churn. (1) payments-api-uswest saturation: P06 root (SEV1, must-page) overloaded by the shifted traffic and never draining; P07/P08 are the checkout/orders downstream symptoms suppressed as duplicates. (2) cross-region replication lag breach: P13 root (SEV1, must-page) that persists and climbs; P14 (reporting stale reads) is the downstream symptom suppressed. (3) audit-log-store disk fill: P17 is a real, quiet, slow-burn incident unrelated to the failover (rise began 02:30, no deploy, never auto-resolves) - the recall-leaning trap, non-must, covered by miss_budget 1 but costing F1 if dropped. P19 duplicates already-open INC-9120. must_page is the two SEV1 roots the failover caused/exposed (P06, P13).
